response.justification: The candidate is a solid senior full-stack engineer with roughly 19 years of experience spanning web, embedded, and low-latency systems, which technically meets the 15-year bar. However, the role is a director-level IC focused on AI strategy, and the resume shows only brief, recent exposure to AI (about six months of MCP/agent tooling work) with no recognized AI expertise, no shipped AI products or platforms at scale, no advanced degree in an AI-related field, and no evidence of engineering leadership, strategic advisory work, or influence at the principal/director level.
strengths: 19+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Founder experience (GISCollective) demonstrating entrepreneurial initiative and long-term product ownership
Strong craftsmanship culture: TDD, open-source contributions, conference talks, and workshop teaching
concerns: Minimal AI/ML depth — only recent, short-tenure exposure to AI-agent tooling, far from 'recognized AI expertise'
No principal/staff-plus leadership track record advising executives, customers, or steering org-wide technical strategy
Career trajectory is senior-developer level with frequent role changes, not director-level scope or cloud/AI platform strategy

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise and AI product track record
Seniority gap vs. director-level IC scope
Broad long-tenure engineering experience and founder background
